topic.para: Colocation constraints use a score to indicate how important the constraint is. A score of INFINITY (positive or negative) means that the constraint must be applied. The score can also be a non-infinite number (positive or negative), which means the constraint is only a preference.
You can add colocation constraints with the &crmshell; (&crmsh;) or with the &hawk; Web interface.

step.para: Log in either as the &rootuser; user or as a user with sudo privileges.
Add a colocation constraint. The basic command requires a name, a score and at least two resources:
step.screen: &prompt.user;sudo crm configure colocation COLOCATION_NAME SCORE: RESOURCE1 RESOURCE2
step.para: The SCORE can be inf, -inf or a number (positive or negative).
The cluster places the last resource on a node first, then places the other resources based on that decision.

listitem.para: You can use square brackets to group resources in a colocation constraint. In this example, res1 and res2 must run on the same node as res3, but aren't otherwise dependent on each other:
listitem.screen: &prompt.user;sudo crm configure colocation col-res1-2-with-res3 \ inf: [ res1 res2 ] res3

listitem.para: You can require a role (Started, Stopped, Promoted or Promotable) for one or more resources. The colocation constraint only applies when the resource is in this role. In this example, res1 must run on the same node where clone2 is in the Promoted role:
listitem.screen: &prompt.user;sudo crm configure colocation col-res1-with-promoted-clone2 \ inf: res1 clone2:Promoted

listitem.para: You can use a node attribute to colocate resources on a group of nodes instead of one node. In this example, res1 and res2 don't have to run on the same node, but must run on nodes with the same value for the datacenter node attribute:
listitem.screen: &prompt.user;sudo crm configure colocation col-res1-res2-in-same-dc \ inf: res1 res2 node-attribute=datacenter

step.para: Check the status to see which node the resources are now running on:
In a Web browser, go to https://HAWKSERVER:7630/ and log in.
From the left navigation bar, select Configuration Add Constraint.
Click Colocation.
Enter a unique Constraint ID.
Enter a numeric Score or select a value from the drop-down list:

listitem.para: Always sets the score to INFINITY, which means that the resources must run on the same node.
Never sets the score to -INFINITY, which means that the resources must run on different nodes.

step.para: From the drop-down list in the Resources section, select a resource.
A new drop-down list appears. Repeat this step to add more resources.

The cluster places the last resource on a node first, then places the other resources based on that decision. To change the order of the resources, click the up arrow icon to swap a resource with the one above it.
To remove a resource from the constraint, click the minus icon.
To create a resource set, click the chain icon to link a resource to the one above it. You can link multiple resources into a set or create multiple resource sets.
To remove a resource from the set, click the scissors icon.
If required, select a role (Started, Stopped, Promoted or Promotable) from the drop-down list next to a resource. The colocation constraint only applies when the resource is in this role.

You can add a Node Attribute to colocate the resources on a group of nodes instead of one specific node. If the nodes have an attribute assigned, the resources in this constraint will all be placed on nodes with the same value for that attribute.
Click Create to finish the configuration.
A message at the top of the screen shows if the action was successful.
